title: Rubedo's AI-Designed Senolytic RLS-1496 Becomes First GPX4-Targeting Drug in Human Trials
description: Rubedo Life Sciences achieved a milestone in precision longevity medicine with RLS-1496, the world's first GPX4-targeting senolytic drug to enter human trials. Unlike repurposed senolytics, RLS-1496 was specifically designed using Rubedo's AI platform ALEMBIC to trigger ferroptosis in senescent cells while sparing healthy ones. Parallel Phase 1 trials are underway in Europe (topical for skin conditions) and planned in the US, with preliminary results showing the drug met its primary safety endpoint. This represents a generational shift from first-gen senolytics (D+Q, fisetin) to precision-designed agents.

title: Longevity ETFs Cap Historic Five-Day Rally: ARKG +15.4% for the Week, All 11 ETFs Green
description: Longevity and biotech ETFs completed a historic five-day rally week. All 11 tracked ETFs posted gains Friday, extending the winning streak from Monday through Friday. For the full week: ARKG surged +15.4%, XBI +7.13%, HTEC +6.99%, SBIO +6.71%, IDNA +6.45%, IBB +4.3%, BBH +3.89%, IHI +1.84%, AGNG +1.69%, THNR +1.58%, and XLV +1.01%. The multi-day rally signals a decisive rotation into high-growth genomics and biotech, adding billions in AUM across the sector.

title: ITP Reports: 11 Compounds Including Astaxanthin and Alpha-Ketoglutarate Fail to Extend Mouse Lifespan
description: The NIA Interventions Testing Program (ITP) published results evaluating 11 compounds in genetically heterogeneous UM-HET3 mice, including astaxanthin, meclizine, pioglitazone, alpha-ketoglutarate, mifepristone, methotrexate, and atorvastatin-telmisartan combinations. None significantly increased lifespan in males or females. Some compounds showed reduced lifespan in females at certain test sites. This is a significant negative result that tempers enthusiasm for several popular longevity supplements and highlights the rigor of the ITP's gold-standard testing methodology.

title: Terbinafine and Miglustat: FDA-Approved Drugs Extend C. elegans Lifespan via Mitochondrial Stress Pathways
description: Researchers screened 770 FDA-approved drugs in C. elegans and identified terbinafine (an antifungal) and miglustat (a Gaucher disease drug) as novel longevity candidates. Both extend lifespan and healthspan by activating the mitochondrial unfolded protein response (UPRmt) without antibacterial activity, and the effects translate to human cells via DAF-16/insulin signaling pathways. As FDA-approved compounds, both are immediate candidates for human aging studies.

title: Kara Swisher's CNN Longevity Series Enters Second Weekend, Driving Mainstream Longevity Awareness
description: CNN's six-episode original series 'Kara Swisher Wants to Live Forever' continued airing its second Saturday at 9pm ET, following its April 11 premiere. The series follows journalist Kara Swisher on a deeply personal journey into longevity science, featuring interviews with leading researchers and entrepreneurs. Combined with Bryan Johnson's mainstream media appearances and the Maryland Longevity Ready Act, the series represents a cultural tipping point where longevity science is transitioning from biohacker niche to mainstream public conversation.

title: Rubedo RLS-1496: First GPX4-Targeting Senolytic Drug Reports Positive Phase 1 Safety Data
description: RLS-1496, designed by Rubedo's AI platform ALEMBIC, met its primary safety endpoint in a Phase 1 trial for plaque psoriasis, atopic dermatitis, and skin aging. It is the first drug to target GPX4 modulation to trigger ferroptosis specifically in senescent cells.

title: ITP Evaluates 11 Compounds in UM-HET3 Mice: None Extend Lifespan
description: The NIA Interventions Testing Program tested astaxanthin, meclizine, pioglitazone, alpha-ketoglutarate, mifepristone, methotrexate, and atorvastatin-telmisartan in genetically heterogeneous mice. None showed significant lifespan extension; some reduced female lifespan at certain sites.

title: Terbinafine and Miglustat Activate UPRmt to Extend Lifespan in C. elegans
description: Screening of 770 FDA-approved drugs in C. elegans identified the antifungal terbinafine and Gaucher disease drug miglustat as novel lifespan-extending agents that activate the mitochondrial unfolded protein response via DAF-16/insulin signaling.

title: University of Arizona Launches Largest-Ever Rapamycin Phase 3 Trial: 720 Adults, $12M Funding
description: The U of A R. Ken Coit College of Pharmacy will enroll 720+ adults aged 65+ to evaluate low-dose rapamycin (likely 8mg/week) for preventing frailty transition and reducing IL-6 inflammation over a 6-year study funded by $12M philanthropic gift.

title: Seragon SRN-901 Published: 33% Lifespan Extension, Outperforms Rapamycin in Mice
source: BioSpace / Drug Design, Development and Therapy
description: Seragon's peer-reviewed data show SRN-901 extended median remaining lifespan 33% in 18-month-old mice on Western diet, outperforming rapamycin (+21%), NMN, and NR. Frailty attenuated 70%, tumor incidence reduced 30.5%.

title: Vitamin C Inhibits ACSL4 Ferro-Aging, Reverses Biological Age 3-7 Years in Primates
description: Landmark Cell Metabolism study identified 'ferro-aging' via ACSL4 as conserved primate aging mechanism. 40-month Vitamin C treatment in aged monkeys reversed biological age 3-7 years on multi-omic clocks, improved brain volume, and enhanced metabolic health.

title: Peter Attia April Research: Paternal Exercise, 40Hz Alzheimer's Therapy, ANS Aging Theory
description: Peter Attia's April roundup covers paternal exercise conferring offspring fitness via sperm microRNAs, 40Hz light-sound therapy slowing Alzheimer's ADL decline by 84% in Phase 2, and a new sympathetic-parasympathetic deregulation theory of aging from Nature.

title: Maryland Becomes First State to Sign Longevity Ready Act Into Law
description: Governor Wes Moore signed the Longevity Ready Maryland Act (HB 278/SB 113), making Maryland the first state to legislate a 10-year multisector plan addressing the impact of longevity.

title: Homoharringtonine Validated as Novel Senolytic via HSPA5, Extends Mouse Lifespan
description: Screening 2,150 compounds identified HHT as a potent senolytic that clears senescent cells via HSPA5-mediated apoptosis. The FDA-approved leukemia drug extends lifespan in both progeroid and naturally aged mice.

title: CNN's 'Kara Swisher Wants to Live Forever' Series Continues Second Weekend
description: CNN's six-part original series following journalist Kara Swisher through the longevity science landscape continues airing Saturdays at 9pm ET, streaming next day on the CNN app.

title: HMGB1 Box A Gene Therapy Reverses Aging Biomarkers in Monkey Study
description: IV administration of HMGB1 Box A plasmid in perimenopausal monkeys reversed age-related proteomic changes, improved bone health, and decreased weight gain over 56 weeks with no adverse effects.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment Hits $3.74B in Q1 2026, Tracking for $8-9B Full Year
description: Q1 2026 saw 49 financing events totaling $3.74B (56% increase over Q1 2025). Analyst Matt Hamilton notes 2026 is tracking to beat 2021's record, with capital flowing into cellular reprogramming, consumer platforms, and cardiometabolic longevity.

title: David Sinclair Debates Elon Musk on Epigenetics vs. Genetics in Longevity
description: David Sinclair publicly disagreed with Elon Musk on X, arguing that studies of identical twins show longevity is 80-90% epigenetic and can be changed by lifestyle. He also highlighted omega-3 plus vitamin D slowing biological aging by 3-4 months over 3 years.

title: Brown University Leads $22M ARPA-H Trial of Censavudine for Aging
description: Brown and Rochester will lead a clinical trial of Censavudine, an HIV drug that quiets DNA-triggered inflammation, in 200+ healthy adults aged 60-65 over 48 weeks. The study aims to slow the biological aging process itself.

title: IGF-1 Signaling Cannot Rescue Mitochondrial Mutator Mice: New Hierarchy in Longevity Pathways
description: Study reveals reduced IGF-1 signaling fails to extend lifespan in mitochondrial mutator mice, establishing that mitochondrial genome integrity is a prerequisite for IGF-1-mediated longevity benefits.

title: UT Health San Antonio Multi-Drug Aging Trial: Rapamycin, Semaglutide, Dapagliflozin
description: Over 700 participants will join a major trial testing three FDA-approved drugs to slow biological aging. NIH-funded Phase 2 rapamycin dosing study (84 adults) runs in parallel. ARPA-H committed $144M to support.

title: Tau Seeds Spread via Brain Connectivity in Individual-Specific Patterns
description: Multimodal study supports tau seeds spreading via synaptic connections in the human brain using postmortem tissues, genotype, and fMRI, ruling out the vulnerability gradient hypothesis for Alzheimer's progression.

title: Bryan Johnson 2026 Blueprint: Data-Driven Protocol, NMN 6x/Week, Lithium, NDGA Added
description: Bryan Johnson's 2026 Blueprint emphasizes data-driven refinements. Key changes: NMN/NR 6 days per week, rapamycin dropped (stopped 2024), low-dose lithium and NDGA added for brain function and cellular support.

title: Longeveron Stem Cell Therapy Shows Promise: 30.8% of Frail Patients Improve to Non-Frail
description: Phase 2b trial of Laromestrocel (Lomecel-B), a bone marrow stem cell therapy from young donors, showed dose-dependent improvement in the 6-minute walk test with 30.8% of treated patients improving to non-frail status.

title: America's Longevity Crisis: Fortune Analyzes the 12.4-Year Healthspan Gap
description: With the oldest boomers turning 80 in 2026, the US faces a 12.4-year gap between lifespan and healthspan. Only 5% of homes have basic accessibility features and 80% of older households lack long-term care resources.

title: Senolytics Pipeline 2026: Field Splits Into Academic, Biotech, and Local Delivery Lanes
description: Comprehensive review finds human proof-of-biology exists for senolytics, but the field is splitting into academic first-gen, biotech pipeline (Rubedo RLS-1496, Unity), and local delivery approaches. Nature Aging compared 21 agents.

title: USPTO AI Senolytic Patent: Neural Networks Trained on 2,352 Compounds Screen 800K+ Molecules
description: Patent application US20260097022A1 discloses AI neural networks predicting senolytic activity across 800K+ molecules, with compounds binding Bcl-2 proteins to selectively reduce senescent cell burden.

title: ARPA-H Commits $144M to PROSPR Program: Seven Longevity Research Teams Funded
description: ARPA-H awarded $144M to seven teams (4 academic, 3 biotech) to validate surrogate biomarkers and build a pathway to regulatory approval for aging interventions in large-scale Phase 3 trials.

title: On Aging 2026 Conference Opens April 20 in Atlanta: Largest US Multidisciplinary Aging Event
description: The ASA's On Aging 2026 conference takes place April 20-23 at the Hyatt Regency Atlanta, themed 'The Power of Belonging' and focusing on advancing justice in aging.
